2400 for the 500 Pro, 800 for the 500 St: 2400 for the 500 Pro, 800 for the 500 Standard.
Enable if your meter uses >0 = feed-in /: Enable if your meter uses >0 = feed-in / <0 = draw.
Foreign state holding the grid power of: Foreign state holding the grid power of your house meter (e.g. a Shelly). Convention: >0 = grid draw, <0 = feed-in.
Local IP or hostname of the SunEnergyXT: Local IP or hostname of the SunEnergyXT 500 (the device must be reachable via its local HTTP API; local mode LM=1 must be enabled).
Log a warning if the grid source has no: Log a warning if the grid source has no update for this long.
Meter serial number for Shelly/Tasmota (: Meter serial number for Shelly/Tasmota (mDNS); the LAN IP for EcoTracker (direct). For Tasmota use the SN prefix without the last 4 characters.
Set GS to 0 (neutral) if the grid source: Set GS to 0 (neutral) if the grid source is stale for this long.
The adapter binds this meter into the st: The adapter binds this meter into the storage (MM=1 + MD) and lets the device regulate itself; it does not write GS. Only the four meter types below are supported, and the meter must be reachable by the storage on the local network. Note: the meter stays usable in ioBroker (it is only polled over HTTP).
The controller writes GS using feed-forw: The controller writes GS using feed-forward + P correction from your grid-power meter state. The adapter forces MM=0 so the device executes GS.
The two control modes are mutually exclu: The two control modes are mutually exclusive; the adapter enforces the matching device mode (MM) and warns if it is changed externally. Requires local mode (LM=1) on the device.
Your Tasmota / BitShake energy-monitor s: Your Tasmota / BitShake energy-monitor subtype (from the device API reference, section 5.4). The adapter derives the correct power key (dat_str.pwr) from it.
• Adapter controller — ioBroker writes: •  Adapter controller — ioBroker writes the grid setpoint (GS) from any meter state; works with ANY meter.
• Device self-regulation — bind a suppo: •  Device self-regulation — bind a supported meter and let the storage regulate itself.
• Off — monitoring only: the adapter ne: •  Off — monitoring only: the adapter never writes MM / MD / GS.
